findings.evidence: `GroupAlgebraProduct.lean:55` has `[CommGroup G] [CommGroup H]`; the construction at `GroupAlgebraProduct.lean:65-66` uses `MonoidAlgebra.prodTensorBialgEquiv` and `AffineGroup.Product.pointsMulEquiv`; `MonoidAlgebraProduct.lean:47` states the bialgebra product under `[Monoid G] [Monoid H]`, and `MonoidAlgebraProduct.lean:145-146` gives the corresponding bialgebra equivalence.
findings.fix: Generalize this declaration and its three simp lemmas to the natural monoid level supported by the APIs, e.g. at least `[CommMonoid G] [CommMonoid H]` for the current `AffineGroup.Product.pointsMulEquiv`, or generalize that tensor-product points API to semiring bialgebras and state this for `[Monoid G] [Monoid H]`; derive the group version as a specialization if desired.
findings.issue: `prodPointsMulEquiv` assumes `[CommGroup G] [CommGroup H]`, but the definition only composes the monoid-level `MonoidAlgebra.prodTensorBialgEquiv` with the tensor-product points equivalence and never uses inverses.

summary: The product-points statement is specialized to groups even though the construction is monoid-level. Prove the general convolution-monoid result first, then keep the group/diagonalizable phrasing as a specialization if needed.
